differences.title: 1. COMPLEXITY
differences.leftDesc: Kubernetes has more advanced features and a steeper learning curve.
differences.rightDesc: Docker Swarm is simpler to set up and manage for basic needs.

differences.title: 2. FEATURES
differences.leftDesc: Offers auto-scaling, self-healing, service discovery, load balancing, and more.
differences.rightDesc: Provides basic orchestration, scaling, and load balancing capabilities.

differences.title: 3. COMMUNITY & ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Huge community support, extensive tools, and a well-established ecosystem.
differences.rightDesc: Smaller community, relies heavily on Docker's ecosystem.

differences.title: 4. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Designed for large-scale, complex deployments and enterprise-grade applications.
differences.rightDesc: Suitable for smaller to medium-sized applications and teams.

differences.title: 5. DEPLOYMENT
differences.leftDesc: Supports declarative configurations via YAML, offering granular control.
differences.rightDesc: Easier deployment via Docker CLI commands, quick setup.

differences.title: 6. NETWORKING
differences.leftDesc: Highly configurable networking with CNI plugins, offering complex routing.
differences.rightDesc: Simplified overlay network for service-to-service communication.

differences.title: 7. MAINTAINABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Requires dedicated teams or significant expertise to manage effectively.
differences.rightDesc: Easier to maintain with less specialized knowledge required.
